reasoning: While the LLM fidelity scores are extremely high, programmatic detectors found critical and major accessibility issues. Most notably, header and footer elements were retained across all 5 pages of the document, which is a critical failure for screen reader accessibility. Additionally, there is a major issue with list flattening. Due to the presence of a critical programmatic defect, the document must be rejected.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (6): PAT MCCRORY
Governor
DONALD R. VAN DER VAART
Secretary
MICHA; State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Waste Mana; Meeting Officer’s Report
Coal Ash Impoundment Classification
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=6, pages_affected=5/5. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (2 list(s)): p1:letter+roman, p5:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=2. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.

In the source PDF, the beginning of each list item in sections II and III (pages 3 and 4) is both bold and underlined (e.g., 'Dam Safety', 'Excavation', 'Beneficial Reuse Areas'). In the extraction JSON, this text is rendered as plain text without any markdown for bolding or any other mechanism to preserve the underline/emphasis. This constitutes a loss of visual emphasis.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Dam Safety – There was a concern... (bold and underlined in source)

action_items: Configure pipeline to correctly identify and exclude header/footer elements
Fix list flattening issue to preserve correct list hierarchies
Ensure visual emphasis (bold/underline) is preserved in Markdown output
